name: Pico Tapinón Circular from Tuiza de Arriba
name_original: Subida al Pico Tapiñon desde Tuiza de Arriba — circular por el Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa
description: Hike to Pico Tapiñon from Tuiza de Arriba, a 6.0-mile circular route in Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa with 2822 feet of gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Lena offers access to a mountainous landscape characterized by prominent peaks, deep valleys, and challenging ascents. The region features diverse terrain, from high alpine areas to forested slopes, with routes often leading to panoramic viewpoints. Significant elevation changes are common, providing strenuous but rewarding experiences for hikers. Best hiking trails around Lena The most popular hiking route is Pico Tapinón Circular from Tuiza de Arriba, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a substantial climb to a peak, offering expansive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is From Refugio Meicín to Peña Cerreos — circular via Tuiza de Arriba, a difficult 5.1 miles (8.3 km) path. This trail involves significant elevation gain to reach a high rock formation, providing a challenging mountain experience. Local hikers also love the Peña Ubiña Pequeña Loop Trail from Tuiza de Arriba, a 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail leading through high mountain terrain, often completed in about 8 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Lena is defined by high mountain peaks, challenging ascents, and scenic valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate climbs to difficult, long-distance routes. The routes in Lena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 45 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Lena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Lena?
A: The Lena region offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate climbs to more challenging, long-distance mountain experiences.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Lena?
A: Yes, Lena has several options for less experienced hikers. There are 7 easy trails available, typically featuring less elevation gain and shorter distances, perfect for a relaxed introduction to the region's beauty.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Lena?
A: Hiking in Lena is characterized by a mountainous landscape with prominent peaks, deep valleys, and significant elevation changes. You'll traverse diverse terrain, from high alpine areas offering panoramic views to forested slopes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Lena?
A: Yes, many routes in Lena are designed as circular walks, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239810377/pico-tapinon-circular-from-tuiza-de-arriba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tapinón Circular from Tuiza de Arriba&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that offers expansive views after a substantial climb.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Lena?
A: The trails in Lena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Hikers frequently praise the challenging ascents, the rewarding panoramic viewpoints, and the rugged, high-mountain scenery.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in Lena?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to several significant mountain passes and summits. You can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt; or reach impressive heights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/825310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Gamoniteiro&lt;/a&gt;. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cristina de Lena&lt;/a&gt;, a pre-Romanesque church, is also a notable attraction nearby.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced mountaineers in Lena?
A: Experienced hikers will find plenty of demanding routes in Lena. Over 20 trails are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and rugged terrain. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238572516/from-refugio-meicin-to-pena-cerreos-circular-via-tuiza-de-arriba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Refugio Meicín to Peña Cerreos — circular via Tuiza de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to a high rock formation.

Q: Can I find mountain huts or refuges along the trails in Lena?
A: Yes, there are facilities like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3728513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meicín Mountain Hut&lt;/a&gt; that can serve as a base or a stopover point for longer treks. These huts are often located in scenic mountain settings.

Q: What is a good long-distance loop trail in Lena?
A: For a longer, more immersive experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238589002/pena-ubina-pequena-loop-trail-from-tuiza-de-arriba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Ubiña Pequeña Loop Trail from Tuiza de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route covers nearly 15 km through high mountain terrain and typically takes around 8 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer views of the Payares Valley?
A: Yes, some routes provide excellent views of the Payares Valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239831033/return-to-the-payares-valley-circular-from-san-miguel-del-rio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Payares Valley — circular from San Miguel del Río&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that allows you to explore this scenic area.

Q: Is there a prominent summit hike in the Lena region?
A: For those seeking a summit experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5516325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueto Negro Summit&lt;/a&gt; is a notable peak in the area. You can access it via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239840155/cuitu-negru-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuitu Negru Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging, long-distance option.
tips: They don't call this part of the road a hill for nothing... the gradient is considerable! This path surrounds the Tapinón to the northwest.
Pico Tapiñon is one of the highest mountains in this area of the Parque Natural de las Ubiñas.

Town from which you can do many routes and of different difficulty.
It has good parking, but if you sleep it fills up.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Ascent to Pico Tapiñon from Tuiza de Arriba trail?
faqs.answer: This circular route is considered moderate in difficulty according to komoot's classification, but trail research suggests it leans towards medium to high difficulty due to significant elevation gain and steep sections. You'll ascend approximately 860 meters over about 9.6 kilometers. Be prepared for varying terrain, including meadows, rocky areas, and potentially less-defined paths, especially near the summit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the hike to Pico Tapiñon from Tuiza de Arriba?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently starts and ends in the village of Tuiza de Arriba. Parking is generally available within or near the village, which serves as the primary access point for this route into the Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed in the Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash, especially to protect local wildlife like rebecos and ground-nesting birds, and to respect other hikers and livestock. Always pack out any waste.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit or to pay an entrance fee to hike the Ascent to Pico Tapiñon trail within the Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa. As a natural park and Biosphere Reserve, it is freely accessible to the public, though visitors are expected to respect environmental regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike to Pico Tapiñon?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the trails are free of snow. Winter hiking is possible but requires specialized equipment and experience due to snow, ice, and rapidly changing conditions.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route to Pico Tapiñon?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers diverse scenery and notable points. You'll pass through the village of Tuiza de Arriba, traverse varied ecosystems from forests to high mountain pastures, and encounter the Mostayar Slope. The highlight is reaching the summit of Pico Tapinón, which provides expansive panoramic views of the Ubiña massif and the Cantabrian Mountains.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and can be demanding. You'll encounter meadows, rocky sections, and steep inclines, particularly through areas like "Las Colgás." The final 400 meters to the summit may have less-defined paths. Hikers should be prepared for uneven ground and potential fog, which can affect visibility and navigation.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter in the Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa?
faqs.answer: The park is rich in Cantabrian fauna. Keep an eye out for species such as rebecos (chamois) on the slopes, and if you're lucky, you might spot brown bears, golden eagles, or Egyptian vultures. The diverse ecosystems also support a variety of smaller birds and mammals.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camín de la Guariza, Camín del Puertu, Camino de Invierno al Refugio del Meicín, and the Caleyón del Puertu for various portions of the hike.

faqs.question: How long does the Ascent to Pico Tapiñon hike typically take?
faqs.answer: The full circular hike is approximately 9.6 kilometers long and typically takes around 4 hours and 40 minutes of moving time to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, pace, and the number of stops you make to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services available in Tuiza de Arriba?
faqs.answer: Tuiza de Arriba is a small mountain village, but it serves as a base for hikers. While extensive services might be limited, you can typically find some basic amenities, and it's a good idea to check for local cafes or small eateries that might be open, especially during peak hiking season.
